source.title: Beta band oscillations in the motor thalamus are modulated by visuomotor coordination in essential tremor patients

confidence_rationale: Human intraoperative recordings from motor thalamus (VIM) showing characteristic beta (13-30 Hz) oscillations that are task-modulated. Directly demonstrates that the motor thalamic relay operates at beta frequency -- distinct from the MD thalamic theta (~7-8 Hz) shown by Griffiths 2022. The two papers together support ARC-023's claim of frequency-specific thalamic nuclei. Confidence limited by patient population (essential tremor) and single nucleus studied.
failure_signatures: Patient population has essential tremor -- thalamic oscillatory dynamics may be pathologically altered rather than representative of healthy motor thalamus.
Motor thalamic beta may originate from cerebellum rather than BG, complicating the mapping to a BG-thalamocortical loop.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The ventral intermediate nucleus (VIM) of the motor thalamus exhibits beta oscillations (13-30 Hz) that are modulated by visuomotor task demands; beta suppression facilitates cerebellar-to-cortical information throughput.
mapping.ree_translation: The VIM beta oscillation corresponds to ARC-023's motor loop heartbeat (E2 at motor command rate). The 13-30 Hz beta band is intermediate between the MD theta (7-8 Hz, E3 deliberative) and sensory relay gamma (30+ Hz, E1 sensory frame rate), matching ARC-023's predicted frequency hierarchy: E1 fastest > E2 intermediate > E3 slowest.
mapping.mapping_caveat: VIM receives cerebellar rather than BG input, so this is technically the cerebello-thalamo-cortical motor loop, not the BG motor loop. ARC-023 specifies BG-like loops with VL as the motor relay. VIM and VL are adjacent and partially overlapping but not identical. Essential tremor pathology may alter baseline oscillatory dynamics.
mapping.source_context: Human essential tremor patients, intraoperative microelectrode recordings during DBS surgery, visuomotor coordination task.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality moderate (Frontiers, small DBS patient sample, disease population). Mapping fidelity moderate: VIM beta is established but this is cerebello-thalamic rather than BG-thalamocortical, and the patient population has tremor pathology. Transfer risk moderate: essential tremor alters thalamic oscillatory dynamics, and VIM/VL distinction matters for mapping to BG loops.
